title: festuca saximontana subsp. purpusiana
intro: Festuca saximontana subsp. purpusiana, also known as Purpus' fescue, is a sub-species of a perennial grass that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Festuca saximontana subsp. purpusiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
